comment_content: Since people don't seem to report back - watched it in almost one go. The show is very good and the most exciting limited series I watched on Netflix in a long time. Seems the bad ratings on IMDb are due to one of the main characters being a non binary person, so don't let this stop you from watching. The series has great characters, looks awesome, has the soundtrack of my teenage years (hello The Vines 💛), Toni Collette's performance is excellent (as expected), most of the kids are super likeable and the show really makes you keep on watching. Each episode was awesome and kept peeling back layers of the past of the characters - very well written and I couldn't predict the ending (which was also good, was scared it wouldn't be). The only thing that bugged me was the communication between one of the main girls and Dempsey - it felt somehow superficial/lacked in depth when it held such importance. Otherwise highly recommended and best watched when dark outside. My favourite limited series (not a Netflix Original but on Netflix and more of an underdog) is The Serpent, btw.

comment_content: Since people don't seem to report back - watched it in almost one go. The show is very good and the most exciting limited series I watched on Netflix in a long time. Seems the bad ratings on IMDb are due to one of the main characters being a non binary person, so don't let this stop you from watching. The series has great characters, looks awesome, has the soundtrack of my teenage years (hello The Vines 💛), Toni Collette's performance is excellent (as expected), most of the kids are super likeable and the show really makes you keep on watching. Each episode was awesome and kept peeling back layers of the past of the characters - very well written and I couldn't predict the ending (which was also good, was scared it wouldn't be). The only thing that bugged me was the communication between one of the main girls and Dempsey - it felt somehow superficial/lacked in depth when it held such importance. Otherwise highly recommended and best watched when dark outside. My favourite limited series (not a Netflix Original but on Netflix and more of an underdog) is The Serpent, btw.

​@foxsterreacts9445 Millennials who lived through the same traumatic tor-ture camps / programs will probably enjoy this show, too. 

I definitely did. 
It reminded me a lot of what I went through,
it haunts me to this day. 

I had to take a few brakes while watching because it brought back a lot of memories 

But I'm very grateful that these places are being finally brought to the public 
And exposed for what they are. 

Most places are actually far worse than what the show portrayed. 

I do wish it had been longer and gone into more detail, 
so that people who didn't go through it could understand better what it feels like 
And all the manipulation that goes on.

Things happens much faster than would have been possible 


I also wish the show had offered some guidance for kids and teens who need help getting rescued from these places 


Adults need to build support systems to rescue kids and teens 


Many adults treat minors like property and don't even see them as humans. 

I feel very deeply for anyone who's gone though this nightmare and who's going to go through it. 

Right now, somewhere, some kid is being kidnapped and brought to a place like this 

The trauma lasts forever, 
most people don't want to believe how horrible these places are. 


Kids need help. Urgently

used as the subject of a verb to refer to people, animals, or things already mentioned or, more generally, to a group of people not clearly described:
I've known the Browns for a long time. They're very nice people.
Where are my glasses? They were on the table a minute ago.
They (= people who know) say things will be better in the new year.
